author: Jessica Park, RN, BSN, CCM (Case Manager) / Diana Mercado, LCSW (Social Worker)
content:
DISCHARGE PLANNING CONFERENCE — 4/8/2027, 1:30 PM (Hospital Day 53)
Patient: Nguyen, Lan T (MRN-90215888)
Location: 5N bed 14, conference room used for family meeting
Interpreter: In-person Vietnamese interpreter (Hopkins Language Services), 60 minutes booked.

ATTENDEES:
- Patient (Ms. Nguyen)
- Son Quang Nguyen (primary family contact)
- Daughter-in-law Linh Tran (joining by phone, on speaker)
- Dr. Anna Reyes (PGY-2 covering medicine team)
- Jessica Park, RN, CCM (case manager)
- Diana Mercado, LCSW (social work)
- Maria Chen, PT (representing rehabilitation team)
- Linh Pham, in-person Vietnamese interpreter

PURPOSE:
Finalize discharge plan including: SNF placement, transportation, medication arrangement, outpatient follow-up logistics, family caregiver education.

SUMMARY OF DECISIONS:

1) DISCHARGE DESTINATION: FutureCare Charles Village SNF.
   - Bed confirmed available 4/12/2027.
   - 10 minutes from son Quang's home.
   - Some Vietnamese-speaking staff on day shift (~30% of week).
   - Anticipated rehab stay: 4-6 weeks for continued PT/OT, transition to home.
   - Insurance coverage: Medicare-covered for up to 100 days of qualifying SNF stay.

2) TRANSPORTATION:
   - Hopkins-arranged stretcher transport van for discharge 4/12 — not needed (patient ambulatory now); will be a sitting wheelchair-van transport.
   - Family will follow in their car (son Quang driving).

3) MEDICATION RECONCILIATION (preliminary, will be finalized day-of-discharge):
   - Apixaban 5 mg PO BID (continue 3 months from initiation; reassess at 1-month hematology follow-up)
   - Lisinopril 5 mg PO daily (BP currently well-controlled)
   - Atorvastatin 40 mg PO daily
   - Acetaminophen 500-1000 mg PO q6h PRN pain (avoid NSAIDs given CKD + anticoagulation)
   - Calcium 600 mg PO daily + Vit D3 1000 IU daily
   - Multivitamin daily
   - Potassium chloride 20 mEq PO daily (continuing from ICU course; will reassess in 2 weeks)
   - Pantoprazole 40 mg PO daily (will discontinue at 1 month if no GI symptoms; was started for stress ulcer ppx in ICU)
   - Insulin: stopped (HbA1c will be checked at PCP follow-up)
   - DISCONTINUED: ibuprofen (CKD + anticoagulation bleeding risk).
   - Pharmacy at SNF will receive paper med rec from Hopkins.

4) OUTPATIENT FOLLOW-UP APPOINTMENTS BOOKED:
   - Dr. Phong Le, PCP (Charm City Asian Health Center): 5/2/2027 — 3 weeks after discharge from SNF (anticipating ~5 weeks at SNF, so this is ~ 1 week post-rehab). Vietnamese-language visit.
   - Hematology (Dr. Pishko): 5/15/2027 — 1 month post-discharge from SNF.
   - General Surgery (Dr. Markovich): 6/1/2027 — for cholecystectomy planning discussion. Will obtain interpreter.
   - Outpatient PT (in case needed beyond SNF): not scheduled; SNF rehab team will recommend at discharge.

5) FAMILY EDUCATION (45 min today through interpreter):
   - Explained: discharge to SNF, not home. Family understood and approved.
   - Explained the anticoagulation medication (apixaban) and the importance of NOT missing doses, NOT taking aspirin or ibuprofen with it, and what bleeding warning signs to call about. Written Vietnamese materials provided.
   - Explained dietary precautions (low-fat, low-sodium): family is committed to home-cooking adaptations. Daughter-in-law will be primary cook.
   - Provided Vietnamese-language written discharge instructions for medications, follow-up appointments, and bleeding precautions. Patient + son have copies.
   - Discussed warning signs for return to ED: severe abdominal pain, fever, leg swelling, shortness of breath, bleeding/bruising, black or bloody stools.
   - Reviewed the importance of staying engaged with outpatient providers — son committed to bringing her to appointments.

6) SOCIAL DETERMINANTS:
   - Medicare + Medicaid dual eligible: all current planning is covered.
   - Transportation to outpatient appointments: son will drive (he has flexible work hours).
   - Language: Vietnamese interpretation arranged at each scheduled appointment.
   - Home environment: 2nd-floor apartment (half-flight of stairs); rehab will work on stair-climbing as graduation goal.
   - Caregiver: son and daughter-in-law are stable, supportive primary caregivers. Daughter-in-law plans to scale back her in-home hair salon work for 4 weeks post-discharge to provide daytime support.

7) GAPS / OPEN ITEMS (to be addressed before 4/12 discharge):
   - Final medical clearance from medicine team day-of-discharge.
   - Final discharge summary to be prepared by the medicine team.
   - Confirmation of insurance authorization for SNF (case management following).
   - Healthcare proxy formalization — patient agreed to formally designate son Quang. Form completed today, in chart.

8) PATIENT'S OWN EXPRESSED PREFERENCES:
   - Patient asked when she could 'truly go home' — answered ~ 4-6 weeks at SNF, then home with continued outpatient services.
   - Patient asked about the cholecystectomy and was reassured it would be discussed after she has recovered her strength.
   - Patient expressed gratitude for the care she has received; family echoed.

FAMILY MEETING ENDED at 2:30 PM. Vietnamese interpreter remained for an additional 15 minutes to walk through the written Vietnamese-language discharge materials with patient.

Next step: medicine team to finalize discharge summary by 4/11; final med rec by pharmacy 4/12 AM; transport scheduled for 4/12 ~11 AM.
attestation:
Electronically signed by:
Jessica Park, RN, BSN, CCM (Case Manager)
Diana Mercado, LCSW (Social Work)
Date: 4/8/2027
